accurate.why: All claims match the bill text (amends 16 U.S.C. 941h(d), strikes 2025 inserts 2030) and the contextual statutory citation is verified correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the statute and date details are accurate per the bill and verified records.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as reauthorizing funding for Great Lakes Basin monitoring, assessment, and research.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the amendment to Section 201(d) of the Further Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2020 (16 U.S.C. 941h(d)).

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a simple date change with no material exceptions or conditions to summarize.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the extension from 2025 to 2030, the key timing provision of the bill.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative provision—striking 2025 and inserting 2030 to extend authorization five years.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, factual description without advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary ends abruptly mid-sentence ('...the bill'), leaving it incomplete and not fully coherent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately brief without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: The statute and codification (Further Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2020; 16 U.S.C. 941h(d)) are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The years 2025 and 2030 and the Senate (Nov. 19) and House (Dec. 15) passage dates are all correct.
